name: Chocolate Malted Cookies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Get out 2 cookie sheets; no need to grease them.
3. Mix together the flour, baking soda and salt; set aside.
4. In a mixing bowl, combine the shortening, brown sugar, malted
milk powder, chocolate syrup and vanilla. Beat for 2 minutes,
then add the egg and beat for a second or two, until mixed.
5. Gradually add the flour mixture to the creamed mixture, mixing
well between additions. Stir in chocolate morsels and chips.
6. Shape dough into 2-inch balls. Place 6 balls of dough onto each
cookie sheet, spacing them 3 inches apart. The cookies are
pretty big, not tiny ones.
7. Bake for 12-14 minutes, or until golden brown. Mine took 12
minutes. Don't over-cook them or they'll be hard as a rock.
8. Cool on cookie sheets for at least 2 minutes or they'll fall
apart. Cool on wire racks.
